zh.lifeSummary: 當Édouard de Bar, Count of Bar於1291年出生在法國洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克時，他的父親Henri de Bar III, Count of Bar31歲，母親Eleanor of England Countess of Bar22歲。他於1310年2月11日與Lady Marie Bourgogne Countess of Bar在法國布格o-達蒙特巴德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1336年11月11日在塞浦路斯享年45歲，埋葬在法國洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Édouard de Bar, Count of Bar于1291年出生在法国洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克时，他的父亲Henri de Bar III, Count of Bar31岁，母亲Eleanor of England Countess of Bar22岁。他于1310年2月11日与Lady Marie Bourgogne Countess of Bar在法国布格o-達蒙特巴德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1336年11月11日在塞浦路斯享年45岁，埋葬在法国洛萊姆斯巴勒迪克。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bar,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Moshe, Giora, Ilan, Arie, Eliahu, Hadassa, Haim, Menashe, Mordechai, Nadav, Nir, Noam.  French Dany, Francois, Henri.  German Hans, Gerhard, Willi.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Bär): from the Yiddish male personal name Ber (see Baer 2).
German (Bär): variant of Baer 1 ‘bear’ and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this and of its cognate Bähr (see Bahr 2).

variants.name: Baer
meanings: German (also Bär): from Middle High German ber ‘bear’, a nickname for someone thought to resemble the animal in some way, a metonymic occupational name for someone who kept a performing bear, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a bear. In some cases it may derive from a personal name containing this element. Compare Bar 2.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Bär): from the Yiddish male personal name Ber, from Yiddish ber ‘bear’. Compare Bar 1.
Germanized form (also Bär) of Sorbian Běr: see Bar 3.

variants.name: Dubar
meanings: French (mainly Nord): habitational name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, denoting someone from any of several places called Bar (see Bar ).

variants.name: Debar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Pierre.
French: habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, denoting someone from any of several places called Bar (see Bar ).

nameMeanings.name: Édouard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Édouard de Bar, Count of Bar was born in 1291, in Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France, his father, Henri de Bar III, Count of Bar, was 31 and his mother, Eleanor of England Countess of Bar, was 22. He married Lady Marie Bourgogne Countess of Bar on 11 February 1310, in Montbard, Côte-d'Or, Burgundy, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 11 November 1336, in Cyprus, at the age of 45, and was buried in Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Édouard de Bar, Count of Bar est né en 1291, à Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France, son père, Henri de Bar III, Count of Bar, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Eleanor of England Countess of Bar, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Lady Marie Bourgogne Countess of Bar le 11 février 1310, à Montbard, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 11 novembre 1336, à Chypre, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterré à Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Édouard de Bar, Count of Bar 1291 in Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lothringen, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henri de Bar III, Count of Bar 31 und seine Mutter Eleanor of England Countess of Bar 22. Er hat am 11. Februar 1310 in Montbard, Côte-d'Or, Burgund, Frankreich Lady Marie Bourgogne Countess of Bar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 11. November 1336 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Zypern und wurde in Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lothringen, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Édouard de Bar, Count of Bar è nato il nel 1291 nel Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia, il suo padre, Henri de Bar III, Count of Bar, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor of England Countess of Bar, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Lady Marie Bourgogne Countess of Bar il 11 febbraio 1310, nel Montbard, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 11 novembre 1336, nel Cipro, all'età di 45 anni, e fu sepolto a Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Édouard de Bar, Count of Barが1291年にフランスロレィヌメウゼバー-レ-ドウックで生まれた時，父Henri de Bar III, Count of Barは31歳，母Eleanor of England Countess of Barは22歳であった。1310年2月11日にフランスボウルゴグンクoテ-ド'オルモントバードでLady Marie Bourgogne Countess of Barと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1336年11月11日にキプロスで45歳で死去し，フランスロレィヌメウゼバー-レ-ドウックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Édouard de Bar, Count of Bar(이)가 1291년 프랑스 로레이인 우스 바-레-드크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henri de Bar III, Count of Bar(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor of England Countess of Bar(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1310년 2월 11일 프랑스 부르고그니 co테-드'어 몽바드에서 Lady Marie Bourgogne Countess of Bar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1336년 11월 11일 키프로스에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 로레이인 우스 바-레-드크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Édouard de Bar, Count of Bar nasceu em 1291, em Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, França, seu pai, Henri de Bar III, Count of Bar, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor of England Countess of Bar, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Marie Bourgogne Countess of Bar em 11 de fevereiro de 1310, em Montbard, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de novembro de 1336, em Chipre, com 45 anos, e foi sepultado em Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Édouard de Bar, Count of Bar родился в 1291 г. в местности бар-ле-дук, меус, лорреаин, Франция, его отцу по имени Henri de Bar III, Count of Bar было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Eleanor of England Countess of Bar, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Marie Bourgogne Countess of Bar 11 февраля 1310 г. в местности монтбард, кoте-д'ор, бургогн, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 11 ноября 1336 г. в местности Кипр в возрасте 45 лет и был похоронен в местности бар-ле-дук, меус, лорреаин, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Édouard de Bar, Count of Bar nació en 1291, en Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia, su papá, Henri de Bar III, Count of Bar, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Eleanor of England Countess of Bar, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Lady Marie Bourgogne Countess of Bar el 11 de febrero de 1310, en Montbard, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 11 de noviembre de 1336, en Chipre, a la edad de 45 años, y fue sepultado en Bar-le-Duc, Meuse, Lorraine, Francia.
